pattern: With at most 16 requests, every subset of requests can be tried directly; a subset works exactly when, for every building, the number of people it sends out equals the number it receives, so the search is just filtering subsets by a per-building balance check.
hints: The requests list is deliberately tiny, so instead of finding clever structure, try every possible subset of requests that could be simultaneously approved.

A subset of approved requests keeps every building's population unchanged exactly when, for every building, the number of times it appears as a source equals the number of times it appears as a destination.

Check subsets from largest to smallest (or track the best count seen), computing each building's net in/out balance for that subset, and return the size of the largest subset where every building balances to zero.

edge_inputs.description: a mix where one request must be dropped to balance every building
self-requests and a small cycle, all achievable together
a perfect cycle through every building, all requests achievable at once
no subset larger than the empty one balances
two independent balanced pairs mixed with one unmatched request

stress.description: near the maximum request count with a random building count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 20)
    m = rng.randint(1, 16)
    requests = [[rng.randint(0, n - 1), rng.randint(0, n - 1)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [n, requests]
